behavior_summary: Provides two gameplay behaviors for a bed object: an inspect/'U8P_BEDNS::look' handler that displays a short description ('bed '), and a use/rest handler that lets the player attempt to sleep/rest for 0–5 periods (4 hours per period).

When used, resting is blocked if the avatar is in combat or enemies are nearby; otherwise the script prompts for a period count, optionally fades the screen, advances the in-game clock by 4 hours per chosen period, restores HP and Mana (inferred from Strength and Intelligence), spawns a helper free process at the start of rest, and restores the avatar's prior stasis state.
notable_control_flow: U8P_BEDNS::look is a short process event (Event 0) that prepares the item and calls process Item::bark with the literal string 'bed '.
U8P_BEDNS::use (Event 1) first guards resting with Npc::isInCombat() and Npc::areEnemiesNear(pid, 1); if either returns true it calls Item::bark('This is no time to rest!') and exits.

If resting is allowed, U8P_BEDNS::use prompts the player with Item::bark('Rest for how many periods of time?'), builds a choice list ['0','1','2','3','4','5'], calls process Item::ask and parses the returned string into a numeric selection (0..5).

If the player selected 0 the function returns without advancing time. For choice > 0 it saves getAvatarInStasis(), sets stasis on (setAvatarInStasis(1)), calls process FadeToBlack(pid), advances time using TimeInGameHours() + (choice * 4) -> SetTimeInGameHours(...), spawns FREE::1C19(pid, 0x64, 0), restores HP via Npc::setHp(Npc::getStr()*2) and Mana via Npc::setMana(Npc::getInt()*2), calls process FadeFromBlack(pid), and finally restores the previously saved stasis value.
open_questions: What exact behavior/finality does spawn FREE::1C19(pid, 0x64, 0) perform during/resting (cleanup, timed regen, or other effect)?
Does SetTimeInGameHours perform hour-of-day wrapping modulo 24 or perform calendar/date progression beyond hour adjustment?
Are HP and Mana set to exactly getStr()*2 and getInt()*2 respectively in all cases, or are there other modifiers not visible in this script (buffs, level-scaling)?
Is the choice parsing strictly string-equals against '0'..'5' (inferred) and are alternate locale/number formats possible?

main_function.name: U8P_BEDNS::look
main_function.purpose: Handle the player's 'U8P_BEDNS::look' or inspect event for the bed object and emit its short description to the player via Item::bark.
entry_conditions: Event 0 (U8P_BEDNS::look/inspect) triggered when the actor inspects or 'looks at' the bed
Fast-area/area-entry semantics treated as an inspect trigger if engine uses area-entry semantics

function_map.name: U8P_BEDNS::look
function_map.role: Inspect handler that makes the bed 'speak' its short description to the player (calls Item::bark).

function_map.name: U8P_BEDNS::use
function_map.role: Use/rest handler that enforces guards (combat/proximity), prompts for rest duration, advances time, restores HP/Mana, spawns a free helper process, and handles fade/stasis transitions.

external_calls.purpose: Display the bed's short description string ('bed ') to the player/process that inspected it.
Guard: detect if the avatar is currently in combat to block resting.
Guard: detect nearby enemies (called with flag 1) to block resting.
Communicate to player: either the prompt 'Rest for how many periods of time?' or the denial 'This is no time to rest!'.
Present the choices ['0','1','2','3','4','5'] to the player and return the selected string for parsing into a period count.
Read the avatar's current stasis/frozen state so it can be restored after the rest sequence.
Set the avatar in-stasis flag (used to freeze the avatar during fading/time advance).
Trigger fade-out effect before advancing time and applying restorations.
Read current in-game hour as the base for advancing time by choice*4 hours.
Set the in-game clock forward by (choice * 4) hours.
Spawn a free/helper process associated with resting (inferred: cleanup/timed effect); called with (pid, 0x64, 0).
Read avatar Strength to compute HP restoration amount (inferred multiplier *2).
Restore avatar HP using a value derived from Strength (inferred as getStr()*2).
Read avatar Intelligence to compute Mana restoration amount (inferred multiplier *2).
Restore avatar Mana using a value derived from Intelligence (inferred as getInt()*2).
Trigger fade-in effect after applying time advancement and restorations.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Provides two gameplay behaviors for a bed object: an inspect/'U8P_BEDNS::look' handler that displays a short description ('bed '), and a use/rest handler that lets the player attempt to sleep/rest for 0–5 periods (4 hours per period).
